ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text content of the tweet */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the tweet was posted */,
  "num_comments" INTEGER /* Number of comments on the tweet */,
  "num_retweets" INTEGER /* Number of retweets of the tweet */,
  "num_views" INTEGER /* Number of views of the tweet */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet (e.g., web, mobile app) */,
  "is_pinned" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the tweet is pinned by the user */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location from which the tweet was posted */,
  "engagement_score" REAL /* Calculated score based on the number of comments, retweets, and views */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Calculated score based on the overall sentiment of the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date the user joined the platform */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* Number of users the user is following */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile image */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Brief bio of the user */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the user is verified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category (for hierarchical categories) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hashtags" (
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hashtag */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that used the hashtag */,
  "hashtag" TEXT /* Text of the hashtag */,
  "frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of the hashtag's use */,
  "trending_score" REAL /* Calculated score to indicate if the hashtag is trending */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hashtag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hashtags_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_hashtags" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hashtag used in the tweet */,
  "weight" REAL /* Importance or weight of the hashtag in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "hashtag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_categories" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category the tweet belongs to */,
  "relevance_score" REAL /* Calculated score indicating the relevance of the category to the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement event */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that was engaged with */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who engaged with the tweet */,
  "engagement_type" TEXT /* Type of engagement (e.g., comment, retweet, view) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the engagement occurred */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used for engagement (e.g., mobile, desktop) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user who engaged */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_engagement_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_engagement_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users_info ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users_info" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the user */,
  "interests" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of user interests */,
  "last_active" TEXT /* Last time the user was active on the platform */,
  "account_type" TEXT /* Type of account (e.g., personal, business) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiments" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment event */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that was reacted to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who expressed the sentiment */,
  "sentiment" TEXT /* Type of sentiment (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the sentiment was expressed */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Score indicating the confidence level of the sentiment analysis */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the sentiment data (e.g., manual, automated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users_info ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiments_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)
